message:
beads: sync issue tracker state

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1503,7 +1503,7 @@
 {"id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.21","title":"Forbid in-place hygiene or repair on suspect live SQLite unless semantic-consistency gates pass","description":"Specify the safety rule that VACUUM, REINDEX, FTS rebuilds, cleanup passes, or any other in-place SQLite hygiene or repair actions are only allowed when the mailbox is already verified healthy and semantically consistent with archive truth. Suspect, stale, broken, or archive-damaged states must route to snapshot serving, candidate rebuild, or explicit safety-hold behavior instead. Include unit tests, shell E2E scripts, and structured logging that prove unsafe in-place repair paths are refused.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-02T21:04:51.054557758Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-02T21:09:32.911683712Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.21","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-02T21:04:51.054557758Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.21","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.17","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T21:07:10.404118434Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.21","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T21:06:40.322863562Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1364,"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.21","author":"ubuntu","text":"This bead turns the safety rule against in-place repair on suspect mailboxes into an enforceable contract. The system must treat VACUUM, REINDEX, FTS rebuilds, and similar hygiene passes as healthy-state maintenance only; once archive truth or SQLite trust is in doubt, the allowed paths become snapshot serving, candidate rebuild, or explicit safety hold. Unit tests and shell E2E scripts should prove those unsafe branches are refused with precise logs and operator guidance.","created_at":"2026-04-02T21:09:32Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.22","title":"Define archive-ahead stale-mode policy: switch reads to snapshots immediately and queue bounded rebuilds","description":"Specify the exact behavior when the archive is ahead of SQLite but the archive is still authoritative and readable. The mailbox must mark this state as Stale rather than healthy, keep reads truthful by switching immediately to archive-backed temp snapshots, avoid user-facing alarm text, and queue a bounded background rebuild or reconciliation attempt instead of blocking foreground reads. Cover how stale mode interacts with owner-broker routing, deferred writes, rebuild admission control, and explicit verification via table-driven unit tests, shell E2E scripts, and structured logs proving that archive-ahead drift never masquerades as health and never causes false-empty reads.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-02T21:12:06.861901380Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-02T21:12:08.528049941Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.22","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-02T21:12:06.861901380Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.22","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T21:12:07.717566664Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.22","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T21:12:07.310166572Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.22","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T21:12:07.501879264Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1365,"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.22","author":"ubuntu","text":"This bead makes the archive-ahead case explicit instead of burying it inside generic degraded-read language. The contract should say that archive truth winning over SQLite is a normal recoverable stale state: foreground reads switch to archive-backed snapshots immediately, users see at most slower reads, and the supervisor queues a bounded rebuild rather than pretending the mailbox is healthy or blocking on repair.","created_at":"2026-04-02T21:12:08Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.3","title":"Detect split-brain owners, deleted executables, and stale live processes before mutation","description":"Treat active mailbox ownership and deleted-binary drift as correctness signals before recovery starts.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:07:03.477766910Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-02T23:32:26.105087074Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.3","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:07:03.477766910Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.3","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:07:03.989862621Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1283,"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.3","author":"ubuntu","text":"Before any mutating recovery path runs, detect:
- whether another Agent Mail process already owns the mailbox
- whether that process is running a deleted executable
- whether the situation requires supervised restart instead of competing recovery

The product should stop treating these cases as weird operator trivia. They are part of the correctness envelope.
","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:07:03Z"}]}
-{"id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.4","title":"Serve all read surfaces from archive snapshots when live SQLite is suspect","description":"Route read-only surfaces through truthful archive-backed snapshots whenever live SQLite is not trustworthy.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:07:04.170007572Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-02T23:55:39.927707301Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.4","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:07:04.170007572Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.4","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:07:04.747829283Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1284,"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.4","author":"ubuntu","text":"This bead is about user invisibility and correctness.

When the mailbox verdict says SQLite is stale, suspect, empty, or broken, read surfaces should keep serving truthful data from archive-backed temp snapshots instead of:
- mutating the live mailbox
- reporting misleading emptiness
- surfacing scary but avoidable failures

Covered surfaces should eventually include CLI, server, web, robot, and TUI reads.
","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:07:04Z"},{"id":1368,"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.4","author":"ubuntu","text":"This bead should name SQLITE_BUSY, lock-starved, or otherwise temporarily unreadable live mailboxes explicitly as part of the 'not trustworthy' class. The user contract is the same as for stale or suspect SQLite: keep reads truthful via archive-backed snapshots, avoid false-empty results, and avoid alarming copy unless the archive itself is the broken authority.","created_at":"2026-04-02T21:14:38Z"}]}
+{"id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.4","title":"Serve all read surfaces from archive snapshots when live SQLite is suspect","description":"Route read-only surfaces through truthful archive-backed snapshots whenever live SQLite is not trustworthy.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:07:04.170007572Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T00:17:10.210959954Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T00:17:10.210256738Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.4","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:07:04.170007572Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.4","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:07:04.747829283Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1284,"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.4","author":"ubuntu","text":"This bead is about user invisibility and correctness.

When the mailbox verdict says SQLite is stale, suspect, empty, or broken, read surfaces should keep serving truthful data from archive-backed temp snapshots instead of:
- mutating the live mailbox
- reporting misleading emptiness
- surfacing scary but avoidable failures

Covered surfaces should eventually include CLI, server, web, robot, and TUI reads.
","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:07:04Z"},{"id":1368,"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.4","author":"ubuntu","text":"This bead should name SQLITE_BUSY, lock-starved, or otherwise temporarily unreadable live mailboxes explicitly as part of the 'not trustworthy' class. The user contract is the same as for stale or suspect SQLite: keep reads truthful via archive-backed snapshots, avoid false-empty results, and avoid alarming copy unless the archive itself is the broken authority.","created_at":"2026-04-02T21:14:38Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.5","title":"Implement exclusive candidate recovery with validation and atomic promotion","description":"Build the recovery runner that reconstructs validated candidates away from the live DB and promotes them atomically.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:07:04.925554239Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-02T21:14:38.494089956Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.5","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:07:04.925554239Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.5","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:07:05.507215885Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.5","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:07:05.735995009Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1285,"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.5","author":"ubuntu","text":"The essential rule here is: no in-place rebuilds of the active DB.

The recovery runner should:
- acquire exclusive mailbox ownership
- reconstruct a candidate away from the live path
- validate the candidate against archive truth and integrity rules
- atomically promote only after validation passes
- quarantine failed candidates without disturbing the prior live state
","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:07:05Z"},{"id":1370,"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.5","author":"ubuntu","text":"Candidate-validation failure should also define the steady-state after rejection: quarantine the bad candidate, preserve the prior live DB untouched, and continue serving reads from archive-backed snapshots while the supervisor either retries within budget or escalates according to the explicit policy. Unit tests and shell E2E scripts should prove this fail-closed behavior across repeated attempts.","created_at":"2026-04-02T21:14:38Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.6","title":"Persist recovery receipts, archive stamps, and quarantine-retention metadata","description":"Record the metadata needed to explain what recovery did and what archive state a DB represents.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:07:05.920191842Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-02T21:05:00.945370343Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.6","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:07:05.920191842Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.6","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:07:06.540104885Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1286,"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.6","author":"ubuntu","text":"Persist enough metadata to answer later:
- which archive head/inventory this DB represents
- when it was rebuilt
- why recovery ran
- which candidate was promoted or quarantined
- what the retention policy is for preserved artifacts

This turns later diagnostics from anecdote into evidence.
","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:07:06Z"},{"id":1362,"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.6","author":"ubuntu","text":"Recovery receipts and DB stamps should be concrete, not vague. This bead should explicitly capture at least old-DB hash, candidate hash, archive HEAD, inventory counts or hash, validation result, quarantine paths, rebuilt_at, and retention metadata, and it should stamp the promoted SQLite state with archive_head, inventory_hash, and rebuilt_at so later drift checks are semantic instead of heuristic. Unit tests and shell E2E upgrade or replay lanes should prove those fields survive across versions.","created_at":"2026-04-02T21:05:00Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.7","title":"Wire supervisor lifecycle through startup, runtime, and upgrade-restart flows","description":"Integrate the supervisor model into actual startup, runtime, and upgrade behavior so the one-owner model is real, not just documented.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:07:06.732576063Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-02T21:12:07.938879209Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.7","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:07:06.732576063Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.7","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:13:49.729051844Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.7","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:20:25.552813958Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.7","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:26:33.421106851Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.7","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.14","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:32:36.792721864Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.7","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.16","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:44:35.343400837Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.7","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.17","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:51:27.509533812Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.7","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.18","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:58:30.423602605Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.7","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.20","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T21:05:51.994880008Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.7","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.21","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T21:08:40.650882564Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.7","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.22","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T21:12:07.937906118Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.7","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:07:07.321326853Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.7","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:07:07.551926708Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.7","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:07:07.798768378Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.7","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:07:08.056851542Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.7","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:13:49.493723761Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1287,"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.7","author":"ubuntu","text":"This bead closes the gap between design and product behavior.

It should ensure:
- startup/runtime flows honor the centralized verdict and recovery ownership model
- deleted-binary and stale-owner situations trigger supervised restart or takeover logic instead of indefinite drift
- upgrade/restart flows stop leaving long-lived deleted executables attached to the real mailbox
","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:07:07Z"}]}
